title: Fernando Noronha Boat Rental: How to Choose Your Charter
topic: how to choose a boat to rent in Fernando Noronha
html:
Choosing the right boat to rent in Fernando Noronha depends on your group size, budget, and itinerary. Departures typically leave from Porto de Santo Antônio, with prices ranging from €150 for shared tours to over €3,000 for a private crewed yacht. BoatHire24 helps you compare verified listings and book with confidence.

Where to set off in Fernando Noronha

Most charters depart from Porto de Santo Antônio or the marina at Vila dos Remédios.

Popular sailing routes include the protected waters of Baía do Sancho and the marine-rich area around Ilha Rata. For those seeking a longer voyage, trips to the neighbouring archipelago of Atol das Rocas are available with the right operator. Many skippers also offer pick-ups from Praia do Cachorro and Praia do Boldró.

Which boat to rent

The ideal choice depends on your group and preferred activities.

  1. Speedboat (small rigid inflatable), best for quick transfers and snorkelling trips; fits up to 6 guests; from €250 for half-day rental.
  2. Sailing catamaran, ideal for relaxed day cruises with family; accommodates up to 12 guests; from €600 for a full-day charter.
  3. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 50), perfect for combining comfort with speed; sleeps up to 8; from €1,200 for a full day including crew.
  4. Luxury superyacht (e.g. the Maiora 26m), offers premium amenities for large parties; up to 12 guests; from €3,000 per day; see this luxury yacht listing for detailed specifications.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Fernando Noronha?

Prices vary widely from €150 for a shared tour to €3,500 for a private crewed superyacht.

  • Shared speedboat tour (4 hours): €150, €200 per person.
  • Private small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €250, €400.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €600, €1,000.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: €2,500, €3,500.

To rent a boat in Fernando Noronha within your budget, compare verified listings on BoatHire24.

When is the best season and do you need a licence?

The high season runs from August to December when the trade winds make for calm sailing.

From January to July, the seas are still navigable but occasional rain showers occur. The local wind, known as the vento leste, provides consistent breezes for sailing. For licence requirements, Brazil generally demands a valid skipper’s licence if you plan to take the helm without a crew. The operator will confirm exact requirements at the time of booking.

For a secluded snorkelling spot, ask to be taken to Baía do Sueste, the shallow waters there are home to green turtles all year round, and you might spot a manta ray if the visibility is good.

At a glance

  • Price band: €150, €3,500 per day
  • Peak months: August to December
  • Typical trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on most private charters
  • Currency: EUR (quoted on BoatHire24)
  • Distances: Baía do Sancho is 2 nm from Porto de Santo Antônio; Atol das Rocas is 38 nm east

Questions about boat rental in Fernando Noronha

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Fernando Noronha?

Yes, you can hire a boat with a skipper included; most private charters come with an experienced crew.

How far can I travel from the main island?

Day trips typically cover up to 20 nautical miles, and longer expeditions to Atol das Rocas cover about 38 nautical miles each way.

What size boat is best for a family of four?

A sailing catamaran or a small motor yacht between 30 and 50 feet offers ample space and comfort for a family.

Is it possible to rent a boat for multiple days in 2026?

Yes, several operators offer multi-day charters, particularly from August to December, when the weather is most settled.
